Setting the Correct Build Path for Vue CLI and Vite

This post covers how to properly set the build path for Vue CLI and Vite. It actually matters a lot — if you don't set it right, both your local dev and post-build paths will break. So here's how to do it correctly.
The build path config is mostly for the build output, but a lot of people set
incorrectly. These two properties shouldn't be set carelessly. Basically, if during local dev your terminal shows something like this:
Then your path is wrong. So this post walks through how to set the build path properly.

Why does the build path need to be set correctly? The main reason is to keep dev and deployment environments separate, which matters most for static asset paths — images, CSS, JS, that kind of thing. So setting it right is extra important.
Let's start with Vue CLI. Usually before deploying a Vue CLI build to GitHub Pages, we create a
property — the value is the path we want, like:
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
is your GitHub Pages repository — mine is
This way both the build path and the local dev path stay correct.
Vite is similar. Usually before deploying a Vite build to GitHub Pages, we create a
This is in the official docs too, but interestingly the
don't suggest a recommended pattern this time.

based on the environment. So you might happily write something like:

instead, and the final version looks like this:
is a Node.js global, we add
at the top of the file so ESLint doesn't complain.
React CRA is similar, but most React CRA setups use
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
So you basically won't run into any issues here.
